purpose: Compact polish map for improving homepage proof and trust without redesigning the homepage or changing its core section order.

referenceBias.notes: Borrow clarity and restraint, not enterprise bulk or portfolio theater.

pageRole.job: Orient the right buyer quickly, establish trust, and route them toward services, work, tools, or contact.

doNotChange: Do not redesign the homepage layout system.
Do not recreate AboutTeaser on the homepage.
Do not replace the services problem-cluster model.
Do not turn the homepage into a long FAQ or pricing page.
Do not add new major sections in this pass.
polishGoals: Sharpen the hero so the commercial model is clearer faster.
Bring trust and proof closer to the top through tighter copy and section handoff, not more UI.
Make the homepage proof teaser feel more selected and less mechanical.
Keep CTA routing consistent and calm.

shouldCommunicate: strategy + systems + websites + growth under one accountable operator
the business problems this model solves
that this is senior, direct, outcomes-oriented work
keep: the hero as the main positioning surface
improve: subhead clarity around the operating model
micro-proof or trust line if it can be added without clutter

keep: routing role rather than deep selling
improve: make clusters read more like buyer diagnosis than category labels
tighten supporting copy so it points more clearly toward the right next click

caseStudiesSectionGuidance.role: selected proof teaser, not a mini-version of the full /work index
improve: make the active proof item feel more meaningful and easier to scan
reduce any sense of carousel mechanics competing with the work itself
bias toward commercially varied proof, not just visually varied proof

bestPlaces: case studies intro or card framing
avoid: dense stats bands that repeat what /work already proves

likelyRuntimeFiles: related CSS Modules directly tied to those sections
antiPatterns: adding a new homepage architecture because proof needs polishing
making the homepage read like a portfolio archive
stuffing more trust modules near the top instead of tightening existing ones
using hype language to compensate for unclear structure
letting the featured tool or proof teaser dilute the hero's core positioning job
successCriteria: The homepage explains the business model faster.
Trust feels stronger without adding clutter.
The proof teaser feels more curated and commercially relevant.
Section-to-section handoff is calmer and clearer.
The page still feels like the same site, just more decisive.
